title: Google Play Console
objectives: Ship an AAB via internal → closed → open → production tracks
Use staged rollouts + Play App Signing
Automate uploads via the Play Publisher API

sections.markdown: The **Google Play Console** is the operator dashboard for Android apps: submissions, tracks (internal/closed/open/production), staged rollouts, Play App Signing, crash + ANR reports, and Play policies.

Two Play essentials: **AAB only** (APKs no longer accepted since Aug 2021) and **Play App Signing** (Google holds the signing key; you upload with an upload key). AAB + Play delivers per-device optimized APKs (smaller downloads) automatically.

Missing target-API-level updates. Google raises the required target SDK yearly; apps not on the current bar can't ship updates. Also: bumping versionCode by inconsistent amounts across CI runs — always monotonically increasing.
Always use staged rollout (5% → 20% → 50% → 100%) with Play Vitals + Crashlytics monitoring. Automate uploads via the Play Publisher API (Fastlane's `supply` or Codemagic's built-in publishing).

flashcards.back: Google Play accepts AAB only (since Aug 2021); it generates optimized per-device APKs automatically.
Google holds the app signing key; you upload with an upload key; key rotation + recovery are managed.

quiz.explain: AAB has been mandatory since Aug 2021.

quiz.q: For a safe production release use…

options: Google holds signing key + you upload with upload key
quiz.explain: Google-managed signing key + your upload key.

challenges.text: Upload an AAB to the Internal track via the console.
Automate uploads via Fastlane supply + a service account JSON.
Configure staged rollout at 5% + halt/resume based on Vitals.
Migrate an app onto Play App Signing without losing upload continuity.

interviewQuestions: AAB vs APK — why AAB now?
Play App Signing — how does it work?
